trade.summary: Solid bilateral trade within the EFTA framework. Both are EFTA members, enabling preferential trade. Switzerland exports pharmaceuticals, machinery, and chemicals. Norway exports petroleum products, fish, and maritime equipment.

diplomatic.summary: Excellent relations as fellow EFTA members and small, prosperous European democracies. Both value multilateralism and international law. Norway's Government Pension Fund Global holds Swiss assets; Switzerland manages Norwegian-related wealth. Both outside EU, both in Schengen.

military.summary: Switzerland is neutral; Norway is a NATO founding member. No formal military ties. Informal security dialogue on European security matters. Norway's Arctic security concerns are outside Switzerland's traditional diplomatic focus.

financial.summary: Norway's sovereign wealth fund (GPFG) invests significantly in Switzerland. Swiss financial institutions manage Norwegian capital. Both are major financial centers in their own right. Strong bilateral investment flows.

energy.summary: Norway exports natural gas and electricity to European markets including Switzerland. Both countries are leaders in hydropower. Norway's offshore wind expertise complements Swiss clean energy goals.

scientific.summary: Solid scientific collaboration through shared EFTA research programs and Horizon Europe. Joint publications in marine science, climate, and life sciences. Exchange programs between ETH Zurich and Norwegian universities.
